- go run .
- Explain packages and main package

Go 1.22+.

Minimal HTTP server /hello handler in Go.

- http.HandleFunc, log.Fatal ListenAndServe
- Test with curl

Standard library only.

Define type User struct and method Greeting() string.

- Pointer vs value receiver one paragraph

- os.Open, if err != nil, defer Close

Contrast with exceptions briefly.

Teach goroutine with waitgroup or channel ping pong simple.

- When to use internal package

Text tree.

prompts.label: Slice length versus capacity
prompts.text:
Explain slices referencing a backing array, and how append can allocate a new array.
Demonstrate inspecting len and cap on a toy example.
Show how sharing sub-slices can create spooky mutation coupling.
Suggest copying explicitly when isolation matters.
Recommend pre-sizing with make when sizes are predictable.

prompts.label: Struct embedding for composition
prompts.text:
Teach embedded structs and promoted methods in Go.
Compare mental models to inheritance and why Go avoids that word.
Warn about embedding name collisions.
Discuss testing with embedded interfaces mock-style lightly.
When explicit composition fields read clearer.

prompts.label: Context timeouts and cancellation
prompts.text:
Demonstrate context.WithTimeout on an HTTP handler calling downstream dial operations.
Show checking ctx.Done in loops.
Explain why goroutine leaks hurt.
Discuss passing context as the first argument as a cultural norm.
Mention cancelling work when clients disconnect.

prompts.label: errors.Is and errors.As wrapping
prompts.text:
Demonstrate wrapping errors with %w and checking them with errors.Is.
Explain errors.As for extracting typed errors.
Show how wrapping improves debugging narratives.
Mention sentinel errors sparingly vs dynamic errors.
Advise avoiding string matching on error texts.

prompts.label: Table-driven tests with subtests
prompts.text:
Construct a small table of inputs and expected outputs.
Explain naming subtests clearly for failures.
Mention pitfalls with shared mutable state between subtests.
Show httptest for handler tests optionally.
Point to benchmarking as optional follow-on reading.

prompts.label: init and global side effects caution
prompts.text:
Explain init execution order pitfalls across packages.
Recommend explicit setup in main for clarity.
Discuss blank-import side effects sparingly.
Relate cyclic import errors and how teams fix them culturally.
Advise beginners to defer advanced init tricks.

prompts.label: io.Reader streaming mindset
prompts.text:
Explain functions accepting io.Reader for flexibility.
Show testing by passing strings.NewReader.
Discuss buffering with bufio for line-based reads.
Mention always draining HTTP response bodies casually.
Connect to graceful resource closing patterns.

prompts.label: sync.Map when rarely
prompts.text:
Explain sync.Map as a niche concurrent map versus protecting a plain map with a mutex.
Give intuition about when niche maps appear in hot caches.
Advise benchmarking before reaching for exotic structures.
Mention atomic.Value for single-value cases lightly.
Reinforce not defaulting to micro-optimization.

prompts.label: go vet in CI pipelines
prompts.text:
Encourage running go vet in CI to catch common mistakes early.
Mention golangci-lint as a broader aggregator.
Discuss race detector on integration tests as expensive but valuable sometimes.
Describe field alignment as optional tiny wins.
Encourage consistent gofmt formatting.

prompts.label: Build tags for integration tests
prompts.text:
Show separating slow integration tests using build tags.
Explain file suffixes like _linux for platform code.
Advise documenting tags in README for teammates.
Warn about drift when tags are forgotten in CI.
Relate to keeping fast unit tests as default.
